original_user_request: Create a 3D Crossy Road game in a single HTML file using Three.js loaded from CDN. Include lane-based roads, obstacles, player character, scoring, and controls.
precise_rewrite: Develop a 3D Crossy Road game within a single HTML file utilizing Three.js sourced from a CDN. The game should feature lane-based roads, obstacles, a player character, a scoring system, and controllable movement.
assumptions: The user desires a fully functional 3D game in a single HTML file.
Three.js is to be used for rendering the game, sourced from a CDN for simplicity.

tradeoffs.name: Speed vs Quality
tradeoffs.rationale: Balancing between development speed and ensuring high-quality features.

tradeoffs.name: Realism vs Fun
tradeoffs.rationale: Deciding whether to prioritize realistic graphics or a more fun, engaging gameplay experience.

tradeoffs.name: Security vs Cost
tradeoffs.rationale: Evaluating the trade-off between ensuring security and minimizing costs.

speed.rationale: The user likely wants a quick development process to start playing with the game.

quality.rationale: While speed is important, maintaining good quality ensures a better user experience.

realism.rationale: Realism is less critical than fun and quality but still important for an engaging game.

fun.rationale: The primary goal of the game is to be enjoyable, so this should have a high priority.

security.rationale: Security is important but not as critical as fun and quality for a simple game.

cost.rationale: Cost should be considered, especially if the project is part of a larger budget.

definition_of_done: The game runs in a web browser without any errors.
All required features (lane-based roads, obstacles, player character, scoring, controls) are implemented.
The game is fun and engaging to play.
Security measures are adequate for the project's requirements.
non_goals: Developing a multiplayer version of the game.
Incorporating advanced physics or complex AI.
Creating a mobile-optimized version of the game.
conflict_rules: If there is a conflict between speed and quality, prioritize quality to ensure a better user experience.
If realism and fun are in conflict, prioritize fun as it is the primary goal of the game.
In cases where security and cost clash, prioritize security to protect user data.
